title: lucilia adpressa
side_effects: Lucilia adpressa can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's sap or leaves can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can lead to liver and kidney damage. Excessive use of the plant's extracts may also result in headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, which can be debilitating and interfere with daily activities. Additionally, Lucilia adpressa can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions like hypertension and diabetes.
